title: Protein in Urine: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
ect at least an ounce or two of urine into the container. The container should have markings to show how much urine is needed. 
Finish urinating into the toilet. 
Put the cap on the container and return it as instructed. 
If you have hemorrhoids that bleed or are having your menstrual period , tell your provider before your test.
In certain cases, your provider may ask you to collect all of your urine during a 24-hour period. A "24-hour urine sample test" may provide more complete results because the amount of protein and other substances in your urine can vary throughout the day.
Will I need to do anything to prepare for the test? 
You don't need any special preparations to test for protein in urine. If your provider has ordered a 24-hour urine sample, you will be given a special container to collect your urine and instructions on how to collect and store your samples.
Are there any risks to the test? 
There is no known risk to having a urinalysis or a protein in urine test.
What do the results mean? 
If your result is negative , your urine did not contain too much protein when you were tested.
If a large amount of protein is found in your urine sample , it doesn't always mean you have a medical problem that needs treatment. Your urine protein levels can also be high for a short time because of:
Strenuous exercise 
Dehydration 
Your diet 
Stress 
Pregnancy
